title: Finding curl in 3D
description: Given a 3D vector field, what is the curl at point (_, _, _)?

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (4x + z, y, -x)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 4x + z \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= y \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= -x
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $0$ | $-1$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $1$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (1 + 1) \hat{\jmath} + (0 - 0) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 0 \hat{\imath} + 2 \hat{\jmath} + 0 \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 0 \hat{\imath} + 2 \hat{\jmath} + 0 \hat{k}$

$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 4z \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= 2x - 2y \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 3y
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $2$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $3$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $4$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (3 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (4 - 0) \hat{\jmath} + (2 - 0) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 3 \hat{\imath} + 4 \hat{\jmath} + 2 \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 3 \hat{\imath} + 4 \hat{\jmath} + 2 \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (2x - 3y, z - x, 5x)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 2x - 3y \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= z - x \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 5x
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $-1$ | $5$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $-3$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $0$ | $1$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - 1) \hat{\imath} + (0 - 5) \hat{\jmath} + (-1 + 3) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= -1 \hat{\imath} - 5\hat{\jmath} + 2 \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = -1 \hat{\imath} - 5\hat{\jmath} + 2 \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (y - 4z, -2x, z + x)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= y - 4z \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= -2x \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= z + x
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $-2$ | $1$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $1$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $-4$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (-4 - 1) \hat{\jmath} + (-2 - 1) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 0 \hat{\imath} - 5 \hat{\jmath} - 3 \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 0 \hat{\imath} - 5 \hat{\jmath} - 3 \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (x + y - z, -4x, 2y + z)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= x + y - z \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= -4x \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 2y + z
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $-4$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $1$ | | $2$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $-1$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (2 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (-1 - 0) \hat{\jmath} + (-4 - 1) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 2 \hat{\imath} - 1 \hat{\jmath} - 5 \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 2 \hat{\imath} - 1 \hat{\jmath} - 5 \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (y^2, x^2 - y, 0)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= y^2 \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= x^2 - y \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 0
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $2x$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $2y$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $0$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (0 - 0) \hat{\jmath} + (2x - 2y) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 0 \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + (2x - 2y) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 0 \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + (2x - 2y) \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (z, x^2 + y^2, x - z^2)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= z \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= x^2 + y^2 \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= x - z^2
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $2x$ | $1$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $1$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (1 - 1) \hat{\jmath} + (2x - 0) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 0 \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + 2x \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 0 \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + 2x \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (x^4 - y^2, z^2 + 3x^4, 2y^2 + 2)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= x^4 - y^2 \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= z^2 + 3x^4 \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 2y^2 + 2
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $12x^3$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $-2y$ | | $4y$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $0$ | $2z$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (4y - 2z) \hat{\imath} + (0 - 0) \hat{\jmath} + (12x^3 + 2y) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (4y - 2z) \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + (12x^3 + 2y) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = (4y - 2z) \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + (12x^3 + 2y) \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (5y^2 - z, xz^2, 2 - xy)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 5y^2 - z \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= xz^2 \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 2 - xy
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $z^2$ | $-y$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $10y$ | | $-x$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $-1$ | $2xz$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (-x - 2xz) \hat{\imath} + (-1 + y) \hat{\jmath} + (z^2 - 10y) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = (-x - 2xz) \hat{\imath} + (-1 + y) \hat{\jmath} + (z^2 - 10y) \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (4z^2, 2xyz, x^2 - y^2)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 4z^2 \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= 2xyz \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= x^2 - y^2
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $2yz$ | $2x$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $-2y$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $8z$ | $2xy$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (-2y - 2xy) \hat{\imath} + (8z - 2x) \hat{\jmath} + (2yz - 0) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (-2y - 2xy) \hat{\imath} + (8z - 2x) \hat{\jmath} + 2yz \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = (-2y - 2xy) \hat{\imath} + (8z - 2x) \hat{\jmath} + 2yz \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (\cos(z), \sin(x + y), x)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= \cos(z) \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= \sin(x + y) \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= x
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $\cos(x + y)$ | $1$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $-\sin(z)$ | $0$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - 0) \hat{\imath} + (-
\sin(z) - 1) \hat{\jmath} + (\cos(x + y) - 0) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= 0 \hat{\imath} + (-\sin(z) - 1) \hat{\jmath} + \cos(x + y) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = 0 \hat{\imath} + (-\sin(z) - 1) \hat{\jmath} + \cos(x + y) \hat{k}$

$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 4 \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= \sin(xz) \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= \cos(y) + \sin(z)
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $z\cos(xz)$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $-\sin(y)$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $0$ | $x\cos(xz)$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (-\sin(y) - x\cos(xz)) \hat{\imath} + (0 - 0) \hat{\jmath} + (z\cos(xz)) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (-\sin(y) - x\cos(xz)) \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + z\cos(xz) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = (-\sin(y) - x\cos(xz)) \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + z\cos(xz) \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (x\cos(z), y\sin(z), z\tan(z))$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= x\cos(z) \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= y\sin(z) \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= z\tan(z)
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $0$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $0$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $-x\sin(z)$ | $y\cos(z)$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - y\cos(z)) \hat{\imath} + (-x\sin(z) - 0) \hat{\jmath} + (0 - 0) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= -y\cos(z) \hat{\imath} - x\sin(z) \hat{\jmath} + 0 \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = -y\cos(z) \hat{\imath} - x\sin(z) \hat{\jmath} + 0 \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (\tan(y), z\sin(x), 2\cos(x))$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= \tan(y) \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= z\sin(x) \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= 2\cos(x)
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $z\cos(x)$ | $-2\sin(x)$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $\sec^2(y)$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $0$ | $\sin(x)$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - \sin(x)) \hat{\imath} + (0 + 2\sin(x)) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ (z\cos(x) - \sec^2(y)) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= -\sin(x) \hat{\imath} + 2\sin(x) \hat{\jmath} + (z\cos(x) - \sec^2(y)) \hat{k}
\end{align}$ 

We could also write the $\hat{k}$-component as $z\cos(x) - \dfrac{1}{\cos^2(y)}$ because $\sec(y) = \dfrac{1}{\cos(y)}$.

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = -\sin(x) \hat{\imath} + 2\sin(x) \hat{\jmath} + (z\cos(x) - \sec^2(y)) \hat{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(x, y, z) = (2\sin(y), \sin(z)\sin(x), z^2)$

$\text{curl}(f) = $ [[☃ expression 1]] $\hat{\imath} + $ [[☃ expression 2]] $\hat{\jmath} + $ [[☃ expression 3]] $\hat{k}$
hints:
$f(x, y, z) = (f_0, f_1, f_2)$

The curl of $f$:

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \det \begin{bmatrix}
{\hat{\imath}} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ \\
\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y} & \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ \\
f_0 & f_1 & f_2
\end{bmatrix} \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
f_0(x, y, z) &= 2\sin(y) \\ \\
f_1(x, y, z) &= \sin(z)\sin(x) \\ \\
f_2(x, y, z) &= z^2
\end{align}$

Let's calculate all the partial derivatives we'll need.

 | $f_0$ | $f_1$ | $f_2$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}$ | | $\sin(z)\cos(x)$ | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}$| $2\cos(y)$ | | $0$
$\dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}$ | $0$ | $\cos(z)\sin(x)$ | 

Now we can put it all together.

$\begin{align}
\text{curl}(f) &= \left( \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial y} - \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial z} \right) \hat{\imath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial z} - \dfrac{\partial f_2}{\partial x} \right) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ \left( \dfrac{\partial f_1}{\partial x} - \dfrac{\partial f_0}{\partial y} \right) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= (0 - \cos(z)\sin(x)) \hat{\imath} + (0 - 0) \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ (\sin(z)\cos(x) - 2\cos(y)) \hat{k} \\ \\
&= -\cos(z)\sin(x) \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} \\ \\
&+ (\sin(z)\cos(x) - 2\cos(y)) \hat{k}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\text{curl}(f) = -\cos(z)\sin(x) \hat{\imath} + 0 \hat{\jmath} + (\sin(z)\cos(x) - 2\cos(y)) \hat{k}$
